# logos-bancos-br

> Dataset + official logos of Brazil's financial and payment institutions (banks, fintechs,
> payment institutions, credit unions), rebuilt automatically every week from official sources
> only: the Central Bank of Brazil (BCB) STR participants list, the BCB active Pix participants
> list, and the Open Finance Brasil participants directory. npm package: `logos-bancos-br`.
> Code and dataset are MIT; the logos are trademarks of their respective institutions,
> redistributed for nominative use (see DISCLAIMER.md).

Key facts:

- The **institution list is auto-updated**: the union (keyed by ISPB) of the BCB STR
  participants list and the BCB active Pix participants list â€” currently ~1,113 institutions â€”
  ships in `data/bancos.json` with `ispb` (8 digits), `compe`/`compe4` (or null for Pix-only
  institutions), official `name`, `shortName`, `pix` (participation attributes verbatim from
  the BCB list, or null) and `logo` (or `logo: null`).
- The **logos are auto-updated** too: currently ~473 institutions, as normalized transparent
  256Ã—256 PNGs (`logos/png/<ISPB>.png`) plus sanitized original SVGs (`logos/svg/<ISPB>.svg`)
  when available. Assets are keyed by **ISPB**, not COMPE. Each logo's `source.type` states the
  origin: `openfinance` (Open Finance directory), `direct-uri` (the institution's own official
  website, hand-reviewed) or `override`. Filter by origin via `bank.logo.source.type`.
  Affiliates of single-brand cooperative systems (Sicoob, Sicredi, Cresol, Unicred) carry the
  system's logo via a curated whole-word brand rule.
- **Update cadence**: a GitHub Action (`.github/workflows/update-logos.yml`) runs every Monday
  (and on demand), rebuilds the list and the logos from the official sources, and opens a pull
  request with a report and the visual diff. Nothing is hand-curated.
- **Per-logo provenance**: each logo records the source URI, the SHA-256 of the original artwork
  and the last-change date in `data/bancos.json`.
- **Matching safety**: a logo is assigned automatically only when the institution's ISPB equals
  the first 8 digits of the Open Finance organisation's CNPJ. Name similarity never assigns a
  logo â€” it only produces suggestions that a human reviews and promotes to `forcedMatches` in
  `pipeline/config.json`. Official-site icons (and AI-scouted domain suggestions) also pass
  human visual curation before being promoted to `forcedUris` â€” nothing ships unreviewed.
- **JavaScript API** (`logos-bancos-br`): `banks()`, `byCompe('341' | 341 | '0341')`,
  `byIspb('60701190')`, `findBank(code)`, `logoCdnUrl(code, { format: 'png' | 'svg' })`.
  Node-only paths: `logoPngPath()` / `logoSvgPath()` from `logos-bancos-br/node`. React Native
  static require() map: `logos-bancos-br/react-native` (keys: 4-digit COMPE and 8-digit ISPB).
- **CLI for any stack** (Flutter, Kotlin, Swift, .NET, PHPâ€¦):
  `npx logos-bancos-br copy --dest <dir> [--by compe|ispb] [--format png|svg|both] [--only 341,001]`
  and `npx logos-bancos-br list [--json]`.
- **CDN, no install** (pin a version):
  `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/logos-bancos-br@<version>/logos/png/<ISPB>.png`. The COMPEâ†’ISPB
  mapping lives in `data/bancos.json`.

## Official sources

- [BCB STR participants CSV](https://www.bcb.gov.br/content/estabilidadefinanceira/str1/ParticipantesSTR.csv)
- [Open Finance Brasil participants directory](https://data.directory.openbankingbrasil.org.br/participants)
